response: The probability for exactly fifteen rooms needing attention is calculated using the Poisson formula with a mean of twenty. This value equals e to the power of negative twenty multiplied by twenty to the power of fifteen divided by the factorial of fifteen. The result is approximately zero point zero zero zero three. For the second part the concierge handles eight concerns with a ninety percent success rate per concern. The chance that at least seven are resolved means finding the sum of probabilities for seven successes and eight successes using the binomial distribution. The probability for seven successes is eight choose seven times zero point nine to the power of seven times zero point one to the power of one. The probability for eight successes is eight choose eight times zero point nine to the power of eight times zero point one to the power of zero. Adding these two values gives a final probability of approximately zero point nineteen six seven.
